smartlead_campaign: OR #6 | Personalized Line | Forecast Lead Magnet | Without Other Contact Name

thread.subject: Braids and Wigs ad report
thread.body_text:
Maria, saw braids and wigs leans on same day shipping plus local delivery in about an hour, curious how that's been landing.

 separate note: I made Braids and Wigs a 4-min ad fatigue report from your live Meta ads. the one about to die, the duplicate eating budget, which to scale, and a fresh angle to test.

 most ecom brands in your niche usually run the same handful of ads too long. this spots it before ROAS drops.

 worth a look?

 Kyle H.
 Co-founder | OmniRocket

 P.S. if there's a better person for this, point me their way :)

thread.subject: RE: Braids and Wigs ad report
thread.body_text:
Maria - forgot to mention: the report also catches the ad Meta is quietly throttling. it's usually a near-copy of another ad that's eating budget without you noticing.

 Braids and Wigs's is still sitting ready on my end - want me to send it?

 - Kyle

 P.S. if it's not you, who should I reach out to?

thread.subject: RE: Braids and Wigs ad report
thread.body_text:
Maria - last check on this.

 should I shelve Braids and Wigs's fatigue report, or is there a better week to send it? no worries either way - just don't want to keep landing in your inbox.

 - Kyle

 P.S. if timing's just off, ping me in a few weeks - still happy to send.

Hi Kyle,
 

 Thank you for following up. Before we move forward, could you clarify what you'd need from us, specifically, did you need access to our Meta ad account to put this report together and if so, what level?
 

 Best regards,
 

 
 Maria Mushi 
 Manager – Braids and Wigs 
 London, Ontario 
 226-504-1321 
 www.braidsandwigs.ca

Hi Maria,

 great question, and the short answer is nothing needed on your end for what i already built.

here's the report btw: Braids and Wigs Ad Fatigue Report 

 this report was built entirely from public data. i pulled your live ad from meta's ad library and analyzed them from the outside, so no account access was involved at all.

 access only comes into play for the deeper work: a full audit plus a 30 and 90 day plan mapped to your actual numbers.

 the way we work is we hop on a quick call first, i get to understand your brand and where you're trying to go, and if we're a good fit we move forward. that's the point where i'd need access, and we'd start with read-only.

 worth a 30 min chat to see if it's a fit?

 Book a time here → Calendar 

 - Kyle

copy.tldr: Braids and Wigs is a value-driven Canadian hair retailer with a big catalog but only one weak 'Send message' ad, no reviews, and a low median price - the whole call is about whether they can bundle up to an order size that carries paid ads.

deep_dive.what_they_are: A Canadian online beauty shop based in London, Ontario. They sell wigs, braiding hair, extensions, and hair and skin care from many name brands like Outre, Sensationnel, Freetress, and Ampro. They also offer same day shipping and local delivery in London within about an hour.
deep_dive.what_they_sell: 500 products across wigs, braiding hair, crochet, extensions, hair care, skin care, makeup, and accessories. Prices run from under a dollar for beads up to about $508. The median price is around $10.52, so a lot of the catalog is small cheap items, but the wigs and human hair pieces run $58 to $145 and up.
deep_dive.positioning: High-variety value play. Name-brand looks at the lowest price, not a boutique premium house. Their promise is bringing beauty to life without the huge price tag.
deep_dive.who_buys: Value-conscious Canadian women who wear and style protective hair like wigs, braids, weaves, and crochet. They want name-brand looks fast, cheap, and with pay-later options.
strengths: Real convenience angle that most competitors cannot match: same day shipping and local London delivery in about an hour, plus buy now pay later.
Deep catalog of recognized brands, so they can lean on names people already search for and trust.
gaps: Only one live Meta ad and it uses 'Send message' as the button. That drives chats, not checkouts, and it reads like a generic storefront post, not a product ad.
No reviews anywhere on the site. For wigs and hair, where fit and quality worry buyers, that missing social proof makes paid ads work much harder.

game_plan.headline: Turn one weak 'Send message' post into real product ads that push people to buy wigs, and lift order size past the free shipping line so the math works.

phases.goal: Find out which product angle and which audience actually sells, not to scale. We want a clear winner between the headband wigs, the human hair frontal wigs, and the braiding and crochet hair.
campaigns: One sales campaign aimed at purchases, not messages, split into 3 ad sets so we can tell the three buyer types apart.
One retargeting ad set for people who viewed a product or added to cart but did not buy, leaning on the money back guarantee and pay later.
lead_products: Human Hair Water Wave Headband Wig - Glueless and Easy Install ($145.12). Lead here because 'glueless and easy install' kills the biggest fear of first time wig buyers, and it sits above the $69.99 free shipping line on its own.
Human Hair Kinky Headband Wig - Glueless and Easy Install ($123.35). Same easy-install story, natural texture, strong for the everyday protective-style buyer.
French Curl Braiding Hair 28 inches ($5.80) as a low-price entry and cart-builder. Braiding hair buyers buy several packs at once, which is how we get the order over the $99.99 free-gift line.
phases.creative: Kill the single 'Send message' post as the workhorse. Build short video and photo ads that show the wig on before and after, focus on the words their own customers use like 'melted hairline', 'glueless', and 'easy install'. Show the price and the pay-later option on screen. One angle per buyer type: quick and easy for the busy woman, natural human hair look for the quality-focused, and value multi-pack for the braids crowd. Add the local London 'delivered in about an hour' angle as its own test since no competitor can copy it.
phases.budget: Start around $50 a day CAD across the three ad sets for the first two weeks, then move the budget toward whichever ad set is bringing sales.
phases.what_success_looks_like: By day 30 we know which product and which audience brings the cheapest sales, we have at least one ad beating the old 'Send message' post, and average order size is trending toward the $69.99 free shipping line or past the $99.99 free gift line.

estimates.label: Starting budget
estimates.value: About $50 a day CAD to start
estimates.basis: Enough to get roughly 30 to 40 clicks a day at typical costs in this category, which is the floor for learning which of the three angles works. Their ad button is currently 'Send message', so we are starting from close to zero real purchase data and need clean spend to learn.

estimates.label: Order size we would push toward
estimates.value: Lift the typical order from around $10 toward the $69.99 free shipping line
estimates.basis: The catalog median price is about $10.52, which is too low to carry paid ads on its own. Their own store already dangles free shipping at $69.99 and a free gift at $99.99, so we build ads and bundles that pull buyers up to those lines.

estimates.label: Revenue we would aim to add
estimates.value: We are not promising a revenue number yet
estimates.basis: We cannot see their current spend, sales, or return on ads. Until Kyle gets those on the call, any revenue figure would be a guess. Once we know their real average order size and current results, we can set a real target.

assumptions: Their real average order is higher than the $10.52 median because wig buyers pull the basket up. If it is truly near $10, paid ads will struggle and the plan shifts to bundles first.
The one 'Send message' ad is close to all they are running. If they have a bigger account we cannot see, the diagnosis changes.
Margins on the name-brand wigs are healthy enough to pay for ads. As a value reseller this is the biggest risk and we must confirm it.
Checkout and pay-later actually work end to end. We only saw the storefront, not a completed order.

questions.question: What are you spending on Meta right now, and who is running it - you, a team member, or another agency?
questions.listening_for: If they spend little or nothing, this is a build from scratch, not a fix. If someone else runs it, find out if they are being replaced or added to.

questions.question: When someone buys, what does a typical order come out to - closer to $15 or closer to $80?
questions.listening_for: This is the whole call. If real orders are near the $10 median, ads are hard and we lead with bundles. If wigs pull it up past $69.99, the plan works as written.

questions.question: On the wigs and braiding hair, roughly what is left for you after you pay for the product?
questions.listening_for: As a value reseller, margin is the risk. If there is not enough room after cost, ads cannot pay for themselves. Listen for whether wigs carry better margin than the cheap items.

questions.question: Have you run ads that pushed people to buy on the site before, not just message you? What happened?
questions.listening_for: Whether they have ever tested purchase ads, and if they got sales, clicks, or nothing. Tells us if the problem is the ad type or something deeper.

questions.question: That ad running now uses the 'Send message' button. How many of those chats actually turn into orders?
questions.listening_for: If chats rarely become sales, the button is the leak. If chats convert well, the message flow is an asset and we keep part of it while adding purchase ads.

questions.question: When people land on a product page, do they buy, or do they drop off? Do you see a lot of add to carts that never check out?
questions.listening_for: Whether the break is getting people to the site or converting them once there. Add-to-cart with no checkout points at price, shipping cost, or trust.

questions.question: You have no reviews showing on the site. Do you collect them anywhere, like in DMs, WhatsApp, or on Instagram?
questions.listening_for: Hidden social proof we can pull into ads. If they have happy customer photos or messages, that fixes the biggest gap fast.

questions.question: What sells best for you already - the human hair wigs, the braiding hair, or the care products?
questions.listening_for: Confirms or changes which product we lead with. If braiding hair outsells wigs, we flip the lead product.

questions.question: How much of your business is local London delivery versus shipping across Canada?
questions.listening_for: If local is big, the 'delivered in an hour' angle becomes a main play, not a test. If it is tiny, we treat it as a bonus.

questions.question: When people search Google for things like 'wigs London Ontario' or a brand like Sensationnel, do you show up?
questions.listening_for: Real opening. People clearly search brand names like Sensationnel and Outre, and 'wigs near me' type searches exist. If they are invisible on Google, search ads and local SEO are a genuine add.

questions.question: Do you get walk-ins or local pickup asking for you by name?
questions.listening_for: If there is local demand, a Google Business and local search push is low cost and high intent. Only push this if local is real to them.

objections.objection: We already get customers messaging us from our ad, why change it?
objections.answer: Messages are great for local chats, but they hide your real sales and cap how big you can grow. We keep what works and add ads that let people buy on the site in a couple of taps. That way you learn what sells and it runs while you sleep.

objections.objection: Our products are cheap, ads will cost more than we make.
objections.answer: That is exactly why we do not push the $6 items alone. We lead with the human hair wigs that sit above your free shipping line, and we build the cart with braiding hair packs toward your $99.99 free gift. Your own store already nudges people to spend more - we just point ads at the same lines.

objections.objection: We do not have any reviews to show, isn't that a problem?
objections.answer: It is a gap, and being honest, it is the first thing we would fix. You almost certainly have happy customers in your DMs and pickups. We pull those photos and messages into the ads so new buyers trust you. It is one of the fastest wins here.

objections.objection: We are a small London business, is $50 a day worth it?
objections.answer: We start small on purpose. The first month is to learn which product and which audience brings the cheapest sales, not to spend big. Once we see a winner, you decide if you want to put more behind it. Your local same-day delivery is an edge no big competitor has, and that is cheap to test.

red_flags.why_it_matters: That is too low to carry paid ads on its own. The plan only works if wigs pull the real average order much higher. If most orders are small items, ads will lose money.

red_flags.flag: No reviews anywhere on the site.
red_flags.why_it_matters: Wigs and hair are trust purchases about fit and quality. With no visible proof, ads have to work much harder and conversion suffers until we add social proof.

red_flags.flag: The only live ad uses 'Send message', not a buy button.
red_flags.why_it_matters: It drives chats, not tracked sales. We cannot see what is working, and it does not scale. We are close to starting from zero purchase data.

red_flags.flag: They sell hair and skin care with claims we must avoid.
red_flags.why_it_matters: Meta restricts health and hair-loss claims. We must never say oils regrow hair, repair damage, or treat skin conditions, or ads get rejected and the account risks trouble.

red_flags.flag: Store shows prices in USD but the business is Canadian with free shipping quoted in CAD.
red_flags.why_it_matters: Mixed currency can confuse buyers at checkout and hurt conversion. Worth confirming what customers actually see and pay.

other_channel_openings: Google search ads on the brand names they carry (Sensationnel, Outre, Freetress, Ampro) and on 'wigs London Ontario' type searches - people already search these by name, so intent is real.
Local SEO and a Google Business profile for London Ontario, since their same-day and one-hour local delivery is a strong reason to capture nearby 'wig shop near me' searches.
